$comment: c59 (D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-IN-ADDITIVE): an ARRAY operand of +/- decays to Ptr<elem> (C 6.3.2.1p3) BEFORE the additive arm types the result, so `array + i` reuses the c41 ptrIntArith stride-Gep and the downstream Deref types correctly. Before the fix `*(array+i)=v` fired H0009 (lvalue-classify on a typeless Deref) and `*(array+i)` fired H0001 (HirKind Deref unresolved). SQLite construct: AtomicStore/AtomicLoad over the `u32 aReadMark[N]` array member (sqlite3.c:68868/69822) == `*(aReadMark+i)=v` / `*(aReadMark+i)`. VALUE-CORRECT + STRIDE-SENSITIVE: runtime index i, int elements (stride 4); a wrong (1-byte) stride or a dropped store would NOT yield 42. a={3,6,9,12}; sum=30; +a[3]=12 -> 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the decay -> the store fires H0009 and the read fires H0001. Pure c-subset -> all 4 targets; pe RUNS on Windows, elf/arm64 RUN on linux/qemu.
